prompt_text:
Analyze the following python source code for violations of the Liskov Substitution Principle (LSP).

Use a TWO-STAGE reasoning process:

## Stage 1 — List design smells (silent, for your own reasoning)
Identify relevant low-level design smells you observe in the code:
- God Class, Feature Envy, Shotgun Surgery, Divergent Change
- Long Method, Large Class, Long Parameter List
- Refused Bequest, Inappropriate Intimacy, Message Chains
- Switch Statements on type, Data Clumps
Think through these int...
raw_response:
{
  "findings": [
    {
      "file_path": "requests/src/requests/structures.py",
      "entity_name": "LookupDict.__getitem__",
      "entity_type": "method",
      "line_start": 88,
      "line_end": 91,
      "principle": "LSP",
      "severity": "high",
      "description": "Subclass of dict returns None instead of raising KeyError and ignores dict storage.",
      "reasoning": "LookupDict inherits from the built-in dict class but violates the base class contract by overriding __getitem__ to return None for missing keys instead of raising a KeyError. Furthermore, it shifts lookup from the dictionary's actual hash table storage to the instance's __dict__, meaning items added via standard dictionary setters are ignored by the getter, breaking fundamental substitutability for the dict type (Refused Bequest smell)."
    },
    {
      "file_path": "requests/src/requests/cookies.py",
      "entity_name": "MockRequest.add_header",
      "entity_type": "method",
      "line_start": 62,
      "line_end": 66,
      "principle": "LSP",
      "severity": "medium",
      "description": "Mimic class raises NotImplementedError for a standard interface method.",
      "reasoning": "MockRequest is designed to substitute for a urllib.request.Request object when interacting with http.cookiejar. By raising NotImplementedError on the standard add_header method, it violates the contract of the interface it mimics. A subtype or substitute should not be less functional than its base, as this causes runtime crashes in code expecting the full interface (Refused Bequest smell)."
    },
    {
      "file_path": "requests/src/requests/auth.py",
      "entity_name": "HTTPProxyAuth.__call__",
      "entity_type": "method",
      "line_start": 107,
      "line_end": 109,
      "principle": "LSP",
      "severity": "medium",
      "description": "Subclass overrides method to set a different header than its parent.",
      "reasoning": "HTTPProxyAuth inherits from HTTPBasicAuth but its __call__ implementation sets the 'Proxy-Authorization' header instead of the 'Authorization' header. This breaks the behavior expected from a subtype of HTTPBasicAuth; a caller expecting the parent's contract (populating the standard Authorization header) will find the program state incorrect after substitution (Refused Bequest smell)."
    }
  ]
}

findings.description: Subclass of dict returns None instead of raising KeyError and ignores dict storage.
findings.reasoning: LookupDict inherits from the built-in dict class but violates the base class contract by overriding __getitem__ to return None for missing keys instead of raising a KeyError. Furthermore, it shifts lookup from the dictionary's actual hash table storage to the instance's __dict__, meaning items added via standard dictionary setters are ignored by the getter, breaking fundamental substitutability for the dict type (Refused Bequest smell).

findings.description: Mimic class raises NotImplementedError for a standard interface method.
findings.reasoning: MockRequest is designed to substitute for a urllib.request.Request object when interacting with http.cookiejar. By raising NotImplementedError on the standard add_header method, it violates the contract of the interface it mimics. A subtype or substitute should not be less functional than its base, as this causes runtime crashes in code expecting the full interface (Refused Bequest smell).

findings.description: Subclass overrides method to set a different header than its parent.
findings.reasoning: HTTPProxyAuth inherits from HTTPBasicAuth but its __call__ implementation sets the 'Proxy-Authorization' header instead of the 'Authorization' header. This breaks the behavior expected from a subtype of HTTPBasicAuth; a caller expecting the parent's contract (populating the standard Authorization header) will find the program state incorrect after substitution (Refused Bequest smell).
